skill.name: error-diagnostics-smart-debug
skill.description: Guides structured debugging, root cause analysis, observability review, fix planning, and validation for software errors.
skill.summary: Diagnose software errors with ranked hypotheses, evidence, fixes, and prevention steps.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The five static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and were false positives from markdown syntax or ordinary debugging text. No executable scripts, external command execution, network scanning, weak cryptography implementation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command finding at SKILL.md line 21 is an inline markdown reference to resources/implementation-playbook.md. It does not execute a shell command or invoke Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks wrap a documentation file path inside an instruction sentence. No executable Ruby, shell, or command substitution context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence
low_findings.description: The external command finding at SKILL.md line 145 is the opening marker for a TypeScript example code block. It is documentation formatting, not shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a fenced code block marker followed by illustrative TypeScript text. It is not an executable instruction or script.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3 matches ordinary description text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing API, encryption code, or security control is defined there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter text describing the skill. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 92 occurs in a list about decision nodes for instrumentation. It does not mention or implement cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is normal debugging guidance about behavior divergence. No weak cryptographic primitive or cryptographic code appears in this context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding at SKILL.md line 132 is the phrase 'Tests pass' in validation success criteria. It does not perform scanning, probing, or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a validation checklist item. No command, tool invocation, target host, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Complex errors are hard to triage when logs, traces, and code behavior are scattered. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through evidence-based diagnosis, fix planning, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Parses issue details, error messages, stack traces, environment data, and reproduction steps.
Creates ranked debugging hypotheses with supporting evidence and falsification criteria.
Recommends observability sources such as logs, traces, error tracking, metrics, and session replay.
Suggests safe instrumentation points, conditional logging, and production-safe validation steps.
Structures final reports with issue summary, root cause, fix proposal, validation plan, and prevention steps.
limitations: Does not include executable tooling or direct integrations with observability platforms.
Requires users to provide logs, traces, code context, or reproduction details.
Cannot confirm a root cause without evidence from the affected system.
Generated fix proposals still require engineering review, testing, and deployment controls.

use_cases.title: Triage Production Checkout Failures
use_cases.description: Use logs, traces, and error frequency to rank likely causes and define a safe validation path.

use_cases.title: Debug Reproducible Application Errors
use_cases.description: Turn stack traces and reproduction steps into hypotheses, breakpoint plans, and focused regression tests.

use_cases.title: Improve Incident Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Convert a solved incident into root cause notes, monitoring gaps, tests, and prevention actions.

prompt_templates.title: Start Basic Error Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a single error report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use error-diagnostics-smart-debug to triage this error. Include the issue summary, likely causes, missing evidence, and first checks: [paste error details].

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Stack Trace Evidence
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate analysis using stack trace and reproduction context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use error-diagnostics-smart-debug to analyze this stack trace and reproduction path. Rank hypotheses with confidence, evidence, and falsification steps: [paste context].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Production-Safe Debugging
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced debugging for staging or production issues.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use error-diagnostics-smart-debug to create a production-safe debugging plan. Include observability queries, instrumentation points, guardrails, and validation steps: [paste incident details].

prompt_templates.title: Build Root Cause Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced post-incident analysis and prevention planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use error-diagnostics-smart-debug to produce a root cause report. Include evidence, final cause, fix proposal, tests, monitoring, rollout plan, and prevention actions: [paste findings].

output_examples.input: A checkout service times out for some users after the latest deployment.
output: Issue Summary: Intermittent checkout timeouts began after the latest deployment and affect a subset of users.
Likely Root Causes: External payment latency, database query regression, connection pool exhaustion, or feature flag mismatch.
Next Steps: Compare traces before and after deployment, segment failures by cohort, inspect slow spans, and run a canary rollback test.

output_examples.input: A local API throws a null reference error when saving a profile.
output: Hypothesis: The profile save path receives a missing optional field and passes it into code that expects a populated object.
Validation Plan: Reproduce with the smallest payload, add a breakpoint at the state mutation, and add a regression test for the missing field.
Fix Direction: Add explicit validation or a safe default before the affected call path.

output_examples.input: A completed incident needs a root cause and prevention summary.
output: Root Cause: A configuration change increased request volume to a downstream dependency without matching timeout and retry controls.
Prevention: Add an alert for downstream latency, document rollback steps, and create a load test for the changed traffic path.

best_practices: Provide stack traces, timestamps, deployment changes, affected environments, and reproduction steps when available.
Validate each hypothesis with observable evidence before changing production behavior.
Pair every fix proposal with tests, monitoring, rollback steps, and prevention actions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the highest-confidence hypothesis as confirmed without evidence.
Do not add broad production logging that can expose sensitive data or overload systems.
Do not skip regression tests after fixing a confirmed root cause.

faq.question: Does this skill run debugging tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance and analysis prompts. Users must run tools and provide results.

faq.question: Can it work with production incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes read-only observability, controlled instrumentation, canary validation, and rollback planning.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide errors, stack traces, logs, traces, metrics, recent changes, environment details, and reproduction steps.

faq.question: Can it generate a fix?
faq.answer: It can propose fix directions and validation needs. Engineers should review and test all changes.

faq.question: Does it support observability platforms?
faq.answer: It references common platform types such as Sentry, DataDog, traces, logs, and session replay. It has no direct integration.

faq.question: Is it suitable for security incidents?
faq.answer: It can help structure evidence and timelines, but dedicated security incident response guidance is still required.
